atoms.detail: Pick a second one now to save a return trip for Shadow of the Storm.

atoms.label: Pick up the strange implement (north-western corner of the temple)
Go to the Digsite (talk to Elissa)
atoms.detail: North-eastern corner of the Digsite; fastest via digsite pendant or gnome glider.

atoms.label: Talk to Elissa about the letter (learn of Varmen's notes)
Search the Exam Centre's south-eastern bookshelf for Varmen's notes
Return to Elissa (ask where the statuette went)
atoms.detail: Points to the Varrock Museum.

atoms.label: Pickpocket Curator Haig Halen for the display cabinet key
atoms.detail: Varrock Museum ground floor; 25 Thieving.

atoms.label: Unlock Display Case 30 and take the statuette
atoms.detail: Upstairs, first row, fourth case. Re-use the key on the empty cabinet if the statuette is lost.

atoms.label: Return to the Ruins of Uzer
Use the statuette on the alcove and turn all 4 statues to face the doors
atoms.detail: East statues face right, west statues face left, all facing north toward the double doors.

atoms.label: Use chisel and hammer on the throne (2 rubies, 2 emeralds, 2 sapphires)
atoms.detail: Optional bonus loot, not required for the quest.

atoms.label: Grind the black mushroom into black dye (pestle and mortar)
Use the phoenix feather on the ink, then on the papyrus (write the Golem program)
Use the strange implement then the golem program on the golem (finish quest)

checkpoints.label: Returning to Uzer: statuette puzzle + program

steps.detail: Bait- or lure-fish the spots east of Barbarian Village for a light, self-caught food supply to feed the barbarian melee-training loop -- earliest-in-position of the 3 food consumers in this route window, so this grounds the cheapest gather rather than an unspecified restock.

mapMarkers.label: Barbarian Village centre -- fishing spots sit east of here, exact tile not pinned on the page

steps.detail: First Elemental Workshop-series quest. Search a bookcase south-west of the Seers' Village bank for a battered book and read it to start. Slash it with a knife to get a battered key, then use the key on an odd-looking wall in the northern anvil-icon building to enter the underground Elemental Workshop. Turn the east then west water controls green and pull the lever to start the water wheel; fix the bellows with leather+needle (Crafting 20) and pull its lever. Mine an elemental rock (Mining 20) to spawn and kill an earth elemental (level 35) for elemental ore (kill extras now to save time for Elemental Workshop II / a Mind shield later). Fill a stone bowl with lava and use it on the furnace, then smelt the ore with 4 coal into elemental metal (Smithing 20) and smith an elemental shield at the workbench with the slashed book in inventory.

req_items.name: needle
req_items.note: obtainable during quest; costume needle does not work

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Search the bookcase for a battered book (Seers' Village)
atoms.detail: House immediately south-west of the Seers' Village bank; the bookcase is on the east wall.

atoms.label: Read the battered book (start Elemental Workshop I)
atoms.detail: Reveals the Fifth Age history of the ore and the sealed workshop.

atoms.label: Cut the battered book open with a knife or slash weapon
atoms.detail: A knife spawns in the house north of the church if none is carried. Produces a slashed book plus a battered key -- do not drop the slashed book, it is needed again at the end.

atoms.label: Enter the workshop (northern anvil-icon building)
atoms.detail: Enter the northern building with the anvil icon; use the key on the cracked wall and climb down the stairs into the workshop.

atoms.label: Turn the water controls and start the water wheel
atoms.detail: Turn the east water control first, then the west one, so both show green (order matters -- reset the west one if it was turned first), then pull the lever beside the wheel.

atoms.label: Repair the bellows with thread and leather
atoms.detail: Bellows are in the eastern chamber. Thread and leather spawn in crates (north-west of the central room, and the western room) if not carried; use both on the bellows (Crafting 20) then pull its lever to start it pumping.

atoms.label: Mine an elemental rock and kill the earth elemental it wakes
atoms.detail: West cavern (Mining 20 to attempt). The rock springs to life as a level-35 earth elemental on the mining attempt -- kill it and pick up the elemental ore it drops (the elementals already walking around do not count).

hints.note: Killing 2-3 extra earth elementals here banks spare elemental ore for Elemental Workshop II and a spare shield -- optional, not required for this quest alone.

atoms.label: Fill a stone bowl with lava and light the furnace
atoms.detail: Search the boxes north-east of the hatch for a stone bowl, fill it with lava from the trough in the southern chamber, then use the filled bowl on the furnace in the same chamber -- the inside should glow orange.

atoms.label: Smelt the elemental ore into elemental metal
atoms.detail: Use the elemental ore with 4 coal on the lit furnace (Smithing 20). Bring 4 more coal per extra shield you want to make now.

atoms.label: Smith the elemental shield on the workbench
atoms.detail: With the slashed book in your inventory, use the elemental metal on the central workbench (Smithing 20) -- quest complete. If the shield option is unavailable, destroy the slashed book and re-fetch a fresh one from the bookcase.

checkpoints.label: Bound under lock and key (Seers' Village -> workshop entrance)
A lost art (smelt metal, smith shield)

steps.detail: Second Elemental Workshop-series quest. Find a beaten book with a scroll in the Exam Centre library south of the Digsite, read both, then head to the Elemental Workshop (same entry as EW1). Search machinery in the water elemental room for a hatch key (must have read the scroll first), and smelt 2-3 elemental ore into elemental metal bars beforehand. Downstairs, collect crane and lever schematics, craft a crane claw from one bar, connect a junction box's pipes per the lever schematic's clue, and find 3 randomly-placed cogs + a pipe piece in crates to fix a broken pipe and set cog sizes on the wind tunnel pins (small upper-left, medium lower-left, large right). Run each elemental metal bar through the crane-lava-press-water tank-fan sequence via a cart-advancing lever to produce a primed bar (do this twice for a Mind shield). One level further down, drain a bar into a primed mind bar at the extractor gun/hat (temporarily lowers Magic by 20 per bar powered). With the beaten book in inventory, smith the primed bar into a mind helmet to finish; a second powered bar plus the slashed book from Elemental Workshop I in inventory instead makes a mind shield.

req_items.name: pickaxe
req_items.note: only if obtaining elemental ore during the quest

req_items.name: elemental metal (or elemental ore + coal to produce it)
req_items.note: 2 metals for the mind helmet, 3 for a mind shield; can substitute 2-3 elemental ore + 8-12 coal

req_items.name: battered key
req_items.note: carried over from Elemental Workshop I

req_items.name: slashed book
req_items.note: only if making the Mind shield

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Search the Exam Centre bookcase for a beaten book
atoms.detail: South-east corner of the Exam Centre (south of the Digsite): search the bookcase two tiles down from the corner, the one attached to both a wall and another bookcase.

atoms.label: Read the beaten book and its scroll (start the quest)
atoms.detail: Read the book then the loose scroll bookmarked inside it. Keep the book for the rest of the quest -- discarding it early blocks the mind helmet at the end.

atoms.label: Get the battered key and slashed book (if not already held)
atoms.detail: House south-west of the Seers' Village bank; search the north-east bookcase for a slashed book and battered key, the same process as Elemental Workshop I.

atoms.label: Search the water elemental room machinery for the hatch key
atoms.detail: Enter the workshop via the key+wall (as in Elemental Workshop I). In the northern water elemental room, search the machinery on the south-west side -- the search option only appears if the scroll was read.

atoms.label: Mine and smelt elemental ore into elemental metal (x2, x3 for a shield)
atoms.detail: Before descending: mine elemental rocks in the west room to wake and kill 2 earth elementals (3 for a mind shield too), then smelt the ore with 4 coal per bar into elemental metal in the south room. Skip this atom entirely if pre-made elemental metal bars are already carried.

atoms.label: Unlock the hatch and descend
atoms.detail: Use the key on the locked hatch in the middle of the room and go downstairs.

atoms.label: Take the crane and lever schematics
atoms.detail: Schematic crate is directly beside an old lever to the south of the entry room.

atoms.label: Smith the crane claw
atoms.detail: Upstairs, use ONE elemental metal bar on the workbench (careful -- the smithing menu defaults to make-all). Do not make a mind helmet here.

atoms.label: Fix the crane, pipe junction, and cog machinery
atoms.detail: Pull the south-west lever beside the old crane and use the claw on it. Upstairs, connect the junction-box pipes per the lever schematic (top-right of the lever to the top-middle supply pipe; the two remaining lever pipes to the press's top-left and bottom-left). Search bottom- and upper-floor crates (near the stairwells, water valve, and schematics crate) for a small cog, medium cog, large cog, and pipe piece -- locations are randomised per player. Use the pipe on the broken pipe upstairs, then fit the cogs on the wind-tunnel pins: small upper-left, medium lower-left, large right.

hints.note: Crate contents (which crate holds which cog/pipe) are randomised per player; keep searching only the crates that yield an item.

atoms.label: Run the crane/press/tank/fan cycle to prime the bar(s)
atoms.detail: Place the elemental metal on the jig cart at the crane; raise, rotate, dip in lava, raise, rotate back, lower then raise again to seat it on the jig. Pull the yellow-sign lever to advance the cart to the press (flatten it with the east lever), then to the water tank (open door, corkscrew the cart in twice, close eastern valve, open western valve to cool blue, reverse to drain, corkscrew out, close door), then to the fan (toggle on then off to dry white). Pull the final lever and collect the primed bar. Repeat the whole cycle for a second bar if a mind shield is also wanted.

atoms.label: Drain a primed bar into a mind bar at the extractor
atoms.detail: One level down, in the mind-symbol room: place the primed bar on the extractor gun and operate the extractor hat. Each bar powered temporarily drains 20 Magic -- only power a second bar if a mind shield is also wanted this trip.

atoms.label: Smith the mind bar into a mind helmet
atoms.detail: Back at the workshop anvil, with the beaten book in your inventory, use a mind bar on it -- makes a mind helmet and completes the quest (without the book, the anvil makes a second mind shield instead).

atoms.label: Smith the second mind bar into a mind shield
atoms.detail: Post-quest only: use the second mind bar on the anvil with the Elemental Workshop I slashed book in your inventory (not the beaten book) to make a mind shield instead of a second helmet.

checkpoints.label: Finishing up (mind bar, helmet, optional shield)

steps.detail: Pick a method: Gold amulet (u) / sapphire ring jewellery / Gem cutting (sapphire, emerald, ruby, diamond).

methods.location: Craft gold bars (with a sapphire for the ring) using the matching mould at a furnace (e.g. Prifddinas or Edgeville).
methods.notes: Gold amulet (u) unlocks at 8, sapphire ring at 20, matching the row's continuity method; page's hourly figures are a templated assumption, not one stated number.

methods.location: Cut uncut gems with a chisel to sell or use in jewellery.
methods.notes: Page frames this as the fastest XP path up to level 77 (sapphire 20, emerald 27, ruby 34, diamond 43); per-cut XP is literal (e.g. 50 for sapphire) but the page's per-hour figure is a templated cut-rate assumption, not a flat stated number.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw jewellery-crafting loadout
atoms.detail: Withdraw a chisel, a ring/amulet mould, and a hammer/needle if not already carried.

atoms.label: Mine gold ore and uncut sapphires (non-GE)
atoms.detail: Mine your own gold ore and uncut sapphires rather than buying bars/gems -- site not pinned by the route fixture (??).

atoms.label: Smelt gold ore into gold bars
atoms.detail: Smelt at any furnace (route names Prifddinas or Edgeville).

atoms.label: Cut uncut sapphires with a chisel
atoms.detail: Chisel each uncut sapphire (Crafting 20+, already held from the earlier route).

atoms.label: Craft gold amulets (u) / sapphire rings until Crafting 40
atoms.detail: Use the gold bar + mould (amulet) or gold bar + sapphire + mould (ring) at a furnace.

steps.detail: Only requirement is 21 Thieving (boostable); no items needed. Track a totem stolen from the Rantuki tribe through a shipping company's Ardougne depot: relabel a crate to redirect it, get teleported by Wizard Cromperty into collector Lord Handelmort's hideout, crack a spoken door password ('KURT' -- type slowly or it fails), thieve open a locked staircase (needs 21 Thieving) and search a chest for the totem, then return it to Kangai Mau in Brimhaven.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Talk to Kangai Mau (start Tribal Totem)
atoms.detail: Shrimp and Parrot restaurant, Brimhaven. Say you're seeking adventure -- he explains Lord Handelmort of Ardougne has stolen the Rantuki tribe's totem for his private museum.

atoms.label: Go to the GPDT depot (west of Ardougne docks)
atoms.detail: West of the Ardougne docks, south of the eastern bank.

atoms.label: Reroute the Wizards' Tower crate to Handelmort's mansion
atoms.detail: Investigate the north-eastern crate for an address label. A separate crate to the south, addressed to the Wizards' Tower, holds Wizard Cromperty's new teleport block -- use the label on that crate to redirect its delivery to the mansion instead.

atoms.label: Ask a GPDT employee to deliver the crate now
atoms.detail: He agrees to deliver the relabelled crate immediately.

atoms.label: Go to Wizard Cromperty's house (north-east of Ardougne market)
Ask Cromperty to teleport you via the block
atoms.detail: Do NOT use the right-click Teleport option -- it sends you to the Rune essence mine instead. Ask what he's invented, then ask to be teleported and confirm; you land inside Handelmort's mansion.

hints.note: Right-click Teleport is a trap option -- use the dialogue path only.

atoms.label: Unlock the password door (K U R T)
atoms.detail: Go west through two doors; the second needs Lord Handelmort's middle name entered with the left/right arrows: K=10 right, U=6 left, R=9 left, T=7 left, then click enter. If it reports incorrect, enter the letters slowly -- fast clicks can be dropped.

atoms.label: Disarm the booby-trapped stairs before climbing
atoms.detail: Investigate the stairs (Thieving 21) rather than climbing up directly, then climb up. Triggering the trap instead drops you into the Ardougne sewers -- climb the ladder out and get another teleport from Cromperty (no need to re-enter the door code).

hints.note: Failing the trap costs a Cromperty round-trip, not a door-code re-entry.

atoms.label: Search the chest for the tribal totem
atoms.detail: Exit onto the balcony via the southern door, enter the room to the east, open the chest and search it.

atoms.label: Return to Brimhaven
Hand the tribal totem back to Kangai Mau
atoms.detail: Quest complete -- he thanks you with 5 swordfish.

steps.detail: One-way point of no return dungeon crawl west of Ardougne toward Tirannwn. Talk King Lathas then Koftik at West Ardougne cave entrance, enter dungeon (no going back except via lever/rope pull). Light an arrow via Koftik's cloth+tinderbox, shoot the bridge guide-rope, cross grid maze carefully (keep HP>15), pull lever. Collect 4 light orbs (avoid wall traps, use plank on flat rocks, disarm trap on 4th), fuel furnace, descend well. Navigate slave-cage/pipe area (50 Thieving shortcut optional). Get unicorn horn via railing-on-boulder trick, kill 3 paladins (safespottable) for badges, use badges+horn on well to open door. Meet dwarves Niloof and Klank (get gauntlets - keep inv slot free), steal Witch's cat to enter her house for loot, kill 3 demons for amulets, brew dwarf beer + tinderbox on tomb. Kill Kalrag the spider (safespottable, ignores Protect Melee), get Iban's dove wearing Klank's gauntlets. Kill a Disciple of Iban for ceremonial robes (required to enter temple), use Doll of Iban on the well in Iban's temple (repeat through his attacks, ~8dmg unblockable spikes) to destroy him. Return to King Lathas via Koftik. Gates access to Tirannwn/Elf lands questline infrastructure.

req_items.name: Non-poisoned metal-tipped arrows
req_items.note: 1-5 recommended; used to light and shoot the bridge guide-rope

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Talk to King Lathas (start Underground Pass)
atoms.detail: 1F of Ardougne Castle. He explains the Underground Pass is the only way to reach his corrupted brother King Tyras, but the cultist Iban has moved in; sends you to his tracker Koftik.

atoms.label: Talk to Koftik outside the Underground Pass
atoms.detail: Through West Ardougne's front gates, west to the cave entrance. Koftik warns the cave corrupts the will of men and tells you to meet him by the bridge inside.

atoms.label: Fire-arrow the bridge rope and cross
atoms.detail: Take the northernmost of the three entrance paths (never the middle swamp path). At the fire, Koftik gives an oily cloth; use it on an arrow, then the result on the fire for a fire arrow. Fire the lit arrow at the rope holding up the bridge to drop it and cross. Do not pull the lever right after the bridge (resets you to the start); take the plank just past it first.

hints.note: Stack a few lit arrows before firing in case the shot misses.

atoms.label: Rope-swing across the dark pit
atoms.detail: South from the plank, avoid the blessed-spider swamp and head east instead. Use a rope on the rock hanging over the pit to swing across.

hints.note: Failing drops you into the pit, loses the rope, and sends you all the way back to the Underground Pass entrance -- bring spares; extra ropes are sold at the Ardougne general store.

atoms.label: Cross the trial-and-error damage grid
atoms.detail: Each wrong tile deals a flat 15 damage into a pit; find a safe path by trial and error, then pull the lever on the far side to open the gate. This exact path is randomised per player but is permanent -- you must retrace it twice more during Regicide, so write it down.

hints.note: Path is fixed per player once discovered; remember or record it for Regicide.

atoms.label: Collect the four orbs of light and destroy them at the furnace
atoms.detail: From the room with the sinister well, four tunnels each hold one orb: north (disarm three traps with the plank), north-west (run past level-39 spiders/level-53 ogres, Protect from Melee helps), middle (disarm traps like the north path), south-west (do NOT pick up the orb until the trap beneath it is disarmed). Backtrack to the furnace near the trap corridor and use each orb on it to destroy them, then descend the now-unlit well.

hints.note: Traps can be disarmed (risk of a 5-14 damage fail) or run through at low current-HP for less damage.

atoms.label: Dig the hidden tunnel and cross the obstacle maze
atoms.detail: West through the slave-cage area; pick the first (south-east) cell's lock and use a spade on the odd patch of ground inside for a hidden tunnel. Edge the narrow ledge, then either cross the raised-beam maze (first right, then left, then left) or -- with 50 Thieving -- pick the gate lock just south of the maze to bypass it entirely. Squeeze through the pipe to the third area. Drop the bow, arrows, spade, rope, and oily cloth here -- none are needed again.

atoms.label: Crush the caged unicorn for its horn
atoms.detail: Search the unicorn's cage for a loose piece of railing (none obtainable if one is already stashed in your bank). South of the cage, use the railing to pry loose a boulder and crush the unicorn, then search the smashed cage for its horn.

atoms.label: Kill the three paladins for their badges
atoms.detail: Sir Carl, Sir Jerro, and Sir Harry (level 62 each, melee only, safespottable by running west) camp north of the well. They first offer food (2 bread, 2 meat pies, a stew, a prayer potion(2), an attack potion(2)) -- then must be killed for their red, blue, and green badges.

hints.note: They will not follow far west of their camp.

atoms.label: Offer the horn and badges at the well to open the Doors of Iban
atoms.detail: West past the traps (plank still useful here) to a second well. Use the unicorn horn and all three badges on it -- the doors of Iban open. Drop the plank now, it's no longer needed.

atoms.label: Talk to the three dwarves (Niloof, Klank, Kamen)
atoms.detail: In the largest area, descend into the small fenced dwarf camp south-west. Niloof explains the corrupted Well of Voyage and points to the witch Kardia; Klank gives Klank's gauntlets (protects against half-soulless bites); decline Kamen's brew offer (accepting costs 4 Agility) -- he'll still sell food for 75 coins either way (named NPC, non-GE).

atoms.label: Return Kardia's cat and loot her chest for the doll of Iban
atoms.detail: Walk the platform paths north of the dwarf camp to Kardia's house; her cat has wandered off along the western path -- retrieve it (yellow minimap dot) and bring it back. Knock on her door; while she fetches the cat, sneak in and search the chest for a stat restore potion, super attack potion, the history of Iban book, and the doll of Iban.

atoms.label: Burn Iban's tomb to imbue the doll with his ashes
atoms.detail: South-east corner of the dwarf level. Take a bucket from the larger dwarf building and a tinderbox from the smaller one; fill the bucket from the barrel of dwarven brew inside the small building, dump it on the tomb, and light it with the tinderbox. The resulting ashes auto-apply to the doll. Drop the bucket and tinderbox afterward.

atoms.label: Kill Kalrag to smear the doll with her blood
atoms.detail: North from the tomb through a blessed-spider corridor (max hit 4) to the level-89 spider Kalrag (max hit 9, fairly inaccurate in melee armour). Heal up before the killing blow -- you're stunned for two seconds afterward while the blood auto-applies to the doll, during which the spiders can pile on.

hints.note: Luring her to the far north or trapping her against the eastern wall jut avoids most spider interference.

atoms.label: Kill the three demons for Iban's shadow
atoms.detail: Level-91 Doomion, Othainian, and Holthion, reached north and west of the witch's house then south. Kill each (safespottable on the narrow pathways) and loot its amulet, then open the chest just north of Doomion's spot -- the three amulets convert into Iban's shadow, auto-applied to the doll.

hints.note: After looting an amulet the matching demon stops attacking and can't be re-attacked -- no need to avoid it afterward.

atoms.label: Search the half-soulless cage for Iban's dove
atoms.detail: Wear Klank's gauntlets first. Head north along the western ledge, across the first broken bridge, then north, east, north, and west to a dead-end cage; search it for the dove's remains, which auto-smear onto the doll.

atoms.label: Destroy Iban at the Well of the Damned
atoms.detail: Loot a disciple of Iban's Zamorak monk robes near the central temple and wear both pieces before entering. Iban attacks with recurring stun-spikes near the entrance; run in with full health and use the completed doll on the well at the centre as soon as possible (may take a few tries if his attacks interrupt you).

atoms.label: Report Iban's death to King Lathas
atoms.detail: Follow the now-sane Koftik back to the entrance, leave the cavern, and return to Ardougne Castle. All quest items are removed on completion -- drop any souvenirs first.

checkpoints.label: Entrance + bridge (Ardougne Castle -> across the pit)
The dwarves + the ritual of Iban (third area)

steps.instruction: Complete In Search of the Myreque
steps.detail: Myreque chain quest 1: deliver steel weapons from Cyreg Paddlehorn's boat at Mort'ton to the Myreque hideout, answer a lore quiz (spelling-checked) to get in, hand over weapons, then safespot-kill a level 97 Skeleton Hellhound before talking your way out. Vanstrom Klause at the tavern is later revealed as the villain -- first appearance in the chain.

refs.title: In Search of the Myreque/Quick guide

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Talk to Vanstrom Klause (start In Search of the Myreque)
atoms.detail: Hair of the Dog tavern, Canifis, north-east corner. Agree to deliver weapons to the Myreque rebels; he mentions a boatman, Cyreg Paddlehorn, in Mort'ton.

refs.title: In Search of the Myreque/Quick guide

atoms.label: Fill the druid pouch with silver-sickled Mort myre fungus
atoms.detail: GATHER-NOT-GE FIX: the page's own item list offers 'buying from the Grand Exchange' as an alternative to collecting charges -- gather instead. Use a (blessed) Silver sickle (b) on fungus-covered logs in Mort Myre Swamp; each fill adds 3 charges to the druid pouch, so 6 fungus tops it to the 5+ charges needed.

refs.title: In Search of the Myreque/Quick guide

atoms.label: Travel to Mort'ton
atoms.detail: On foot through Mort Myre Swamp (ghasts drain druid pouch charges en route -- the extra fungus covers refills), or with 50+ Agility and fairy rings unlocked, ring code bip then the agility shortcut south of the swamp.

refs.title: In Search of the Myreque/Quick guide

atoms.label: Persuade Cyreg Paddlehorn to ferry you across
atoms.detail: Eastern edge of Mort'ton, by his boat at the Barrows bridge. With all five steel weapons and a 5+-charge druid pouch in your inventory, choose the four-step guilt-trip dialogue chain in order (own words: 'they'll die without weapons' -> 'resourceful enough for their own steel?' -> 'their deaths are on your head' -> 'what kind of man says that'). He asks for 3 planks to fix his boat and 3 more for a bridge, then ferries you to The Hollows for 10 coins (waived with a ring of charos (a)).

hints.note: Exact order matters; each response escalates the guilt angle.

refs.title: In Search of the Myreque/Quick guide

atoms.label: Repair the rope bridge into the Hollows
atoms.detail: North from the landing, avoiding ghasts, to a damaged rope bridge in a strange tree. Mend it while crossing with 3 planks and 225 steel nails (75 per plank), then climb down the far side.

refs.title: In Search of the Myreque/Quick guide

atoms.label: Answer Curpile Fyod's loyalty question
atoms.detail: North to the hollow tree. Tell Curpile you've brought weapons; he asks one randomised question to confirm you're telling the truth (own-words answer key: only female member = Sani Piliu; Mort Myre leader = Veliaf Hurtz; the boatman = Cyreg Paddlehorn; the scholar member = Polmafi Ferdygris; the youngest member = Ivan Strom; the ruling vampyre family = Drakan). A wrong answer knocks you out at the boat -- the bridge stays fixed and the pouch charge requirement is waived on retry.

hints.note: One of six possible questions, randomised per attempt.

refs.title: In Search of the Myreque/Quick guide

atoms.label: Introduce yourself to the five Myreque members
atoms.detail: Circle north to the hidden south-east alcove cave entrance. Talk to Veliaf first, then briefly greet each of the other five members ('I'll be back later' is enough -- no follow-up questions required).

refs.title: In Search of the Myreque/Quick guide

atoms.label: Kill the level-97 Skeleton Hellhound
atoms.detail: Return to Veliaf and hand over the five steel weapons -- a cutscene reveals Vanstrom Klause as a vampyre, who kills two members and summons a 55-HP hellhound (no Magic defence, weak to Earth). Kill it; there is a safespot in the upper-east corner behind the chair.

refs.title: In Search of the Myreque/Quick guide

atoms.label: Ask Veliaf how to leave the hideout
atoms.detail: He reveals a secret passage to Canifis.

refs.title: In Search of the Myreque/Quick guide

atoms.label: Exit via the secret passage to Canifis
atoms.detail: Search the wall at the end of the tunnel to open it, then climb the ladder up behind the Canifis tavern. Exiting via any other route requires a repeat trip to progress the quest.

refs.title: In Search of the Myreque/Quick guide

atoms.label: Talk to the Stranger in the tavern to finish
atoms.detail: Vanstrom is gone, replaced by a nondescript Stranger. Quest complete.

refs.title: In Search of the Myreque/Quick guide

steps.detail: Intermediate quest requiring defeat of a level 172 Black demon (safespottable). Talk to King Shareen for a translation book and bark sample, deliver the sample to Hazelmere on the island east of Yanille (fairy ring CLS) for a message. Report back and tell the king about the man with the King's seal and the Daconia rocks; talk to imprisoned Charlie upstairs, search Glough's house cupboard for his journal, confront Glough (he arrests you), get the shipyard password 'Ka-Lu-Min' from Charlie, and escape via the gnome glider. On Karamja: run past level 53 jogres to the Ship Yard, give the password, get a lumber order from the Foreman (fight him or answer his security questions), and deliver the order to the king. Talk to Charlie again, get a key from Anita north-west of the tree, take invasion plans from Glough's chest, deliver them for 4 sticks, spell TUZO on the watchtower pillars in Glough's house, then descend to fight a level 172 Black demon (lure it against the daconia rocks north of the ladder for a safespot). Find the last daconia rock by searching roots, then finish by talking to the king. Required for: Monkey Madness I, The Eyes of Glouphrie.

req_items.name: Coins
req_items.note: only needed if you previously refused to help Femi at the Stronghold gate

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Talk to King Narnode Shareen (start The Grand Tree)
atoms.detail: Ground floor of the Grand Tree, with 2 free inventory slots. He shows you the dying roots underground and gives a translation book and a bark sample, asking you to visit Hazelmere.

atoms.label: Travel to Hazelmere's house
atoms.detail: Small island east of Yanille, upstairs. Level-44 jungle spiders roam the path but are not poisonous.

atoms.label: Give Hazelmere the bark sample
atoms.detail: He examines the bark and gives back a message written in his own language.

atoms.label: Decode the message for King Narnode
atoms.detail: Use the translation book to decode Hazelmere's message ('a man came with the King's seal; I gave him Daconia rocks; Daconia rocks will kill the tree'). Select 'None of the above' for the first two dialogue prompts, then the three real lines in order. Narnode realises his seal was forged and sends you to his head tree guardian, Glough.

atoms.label: Talk to Glough
atoms.detail: Treehouse south of the Grand Tree, east of the balloon ramp. He dismisses humans as untrustworthy and says he'll handle it.

atoms.label: Talk to Charlie the prisoner
atoms.detail: Narnode reveals Glough imprisoned a human found with Daconia rocks. Top floor of the Grand Tree: Charlie says Glough paid him to steal from Hazelmere and suggests searching Glough's house.

atoms.label: Search Glough's cupboard for his journal
atoms.detail: The journal details a plan to frame humans as a threat to the tree gnomes. Confronting Glough with it gets you thrown into the cell beside Charlie's; Charlie gives the shipyard password 'Ka-Lu-Min'.

atoms.label: Escape to Karamja on the Gnome glider
atoms.detail: Narnode apologises and points you to the top-floor glider beside the cells. Ask the pilot to fly you to Karamja.

atoms.label: Get the lumber order from the Shipyard foreman
atoms.detail: The glider crash-lands west of the Shipyard. Run east past aggressive level-53 jogres; tell the gate worker Glough sent you plus the password. Talk to the level-23 foreman on the south docks, say Glough sent you, and answer his three personal questions (own-words key: his late wife -> 'Sadly his wife is no longer with us'; favourite dish -> 'He loves worm holes'; girlfriend's name -> 'Anita') to get a lumber order.

atoms.label: Sneak back into the Tree Gnome Stronghold
atoms.detail: Glough has barred the gate guards from letting you in. If Tree Gnome Village is done, use the Battlefield or Grand Exchange spirit tree instead. Otherwise talk to Femi by the gate -- free if never entered via the gate before, or if you helped her move boxes on first entry; 1,000 coins if you previously declined.

atoms.label: Show King Narnode the lumber order
atoms.detail: He still doubts you and sends you away for your own safety, but Charlie later mentions Glough's chest key is with his girlfriend Anita.

atoms.label: Fetch Glough's key from Anita and open his chest
atoms.detail: Climb the north-western-most ladder by the tortoise pens to talk to Anita for Glough's key. Use the key on the chest in Glough's house and click continue to receive his invasion plans.

atoms.label: Spell TUZO on the watchtower pillars
atoms.detail: Ensure 4 free inventory slots; bring the plans to King Narnode (still unconvinced, but gives 4 bundles of twigs). Climb the east watchtower in Glough's house (Agility 25) and use the twigs on the four pillars to spell TUZO (translation-book word for 'open'), starting from the far left. This unlocks the trapdoor -- do not descend until ready for the demon fight.

atoms.label: Kill the level-172 black demon
atoms.detail: Climb down; complete Glough's threatening dialogue in full without interacting with anything else, or you'll have to repeat it. The demon (40% weak to water spells, negative Magic defence) can be safespotted one tile north then east of the ladder. If not killed within 10 minutes it disappears and the fight must be restarted from the top-floor ladder.

hints.note: 10-minute timer resets the fight if not killed in time.

atoms.label: Search the roots for the last Daconia rock
atoms.detail: After the fight, a guard finds Glough hiding and Narnode apologises. Ensure 1 free inventory slot, then search the roots around the area until the rock turns up -- click continue to claim it.

atoms.label: Return the Daconia rock to King Narnode
atoms.detail: Quest complete.

checkpoints.label: Encountering the Black demon + the adamant king

steps.detail: Pick a method: Superheat Item / Curse-tier spell splashing / Enchant Crossbow Bolts (Emerald bolts) / Grand Library of Great Kourend (book delivery).

hints.note: Superheat Item gives passive Smithing xp on the bar produced.

methods.location: Any bank with ores nearby (e.g. Falador east, Edgeville)
methods.notes: Unlocks at 43, the top of this band; also grants passive Smithing xp on the bar produced, matching the row's own detail.

methods.location: Any safe weak monster with Auto Retaliate on

items: gear with -64 or lower Magic attack bonus

methods.notes: Matches the row's own 'Curse spells' label via the page's splashing technique: no damage dealt but base cast xp is still granted, needing input roughly once every 20 minutes. Page states a 6,600-75,750 xp/h range depending on spell, not a single figure.

methods.location: Bank with unenchanted emerald bolts + cosmic/air/nature runes
methods.notes: Continues the bolt-enchant ladder into this band; topaz bolts (level 29, 181,500 xp/h) are a cheaper alternative from the same table.

methods.method: Grand Library of Great Kourend (book delivery)
methods.location: Arceuus Library, Great Kourend
methods.notes: No rune cost; page states each delivered book grants 15x the player's current Magic level in xp, so per-hour totals scale with level rather than being one fixed number. No minimum level to start.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw Superheat Item loadout
atoms.detail: Withdraw nature runes (1/cast) and fire runes (4/cast) per Superheat Item's own cost table, plus banked iron ore + coal.

atoms.label: Mine iron ore and coal (non-GE)
atoms.detail: Steel bar needs 1 iron ore + 2 coal per bar (Steel bar's own Recipe). Mine both -- site not pinned by the route fixture (??); xref train-mining-45 later in this same segment trains Mining on iron ore at a triangle site, a plausible shared source once Mining is high enough.

atoms.label: Cast Superheat Item on iron ore until Magic 43
atoms.detail: 1 iron ore + 2 coal + 1 nature rune + 4 fire runes per cast -> 1 steel bar, per Steel bar's own Superheat recipe row. Also grants passive Smithing xp on the bar produced.

hints.note: Superheat Item gives passive Smithing xp on the bar produced -- xref: training:train-smithing-40 (same bars, shared recipe).

steps.detail: Novice Hunter-skill quest, no series. Charlie the zookeeper needs help finding huntsman Nickolaus, missing near Eagles' Peak west of the Tree Gnome Stronghold. Find his tent, pick up a bird book that drops a metal feather, and use it to open the mountain-top door into Eagles' Peak Dungeon. Shout to Nickolaus, trapped by a giant eagle, then craft two eagle costumes (10 eagle feathers + swamp tar + yellow dye + 50 coins at Varrock's Fancy Dress Shop) so you can both disguise as eagles. To open the eagle door to Nickolaus you first need three feathers: bronze (defeat a net trap via four corner winches), silver (Hunter 27 to read a pedestal trail leading to a hidden tunnel, then kill or threaten a level 13 kebbit), and gold (solve a bird-seed/lever puzzle moving eagle statues and wing-gates through a specific lever/feeder sequence, reset lever available if you mess it up). Use all three feathers on the eagle door, wear the disguise to pass the guard eagle, then learn to catch ferrets from Nickolaus (no box trap needed for this part) and deliver a ferret to Charlie at Ardougne Zoo to finish.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Talk to Charlie (start Eagles' Peak)
atoms.detail: South-west part of Ardougne Zoo, south of the scorpion cage. He explains a delivered ferret escaped and the huntsman sent to recapture one, Nickolaus, has gone quiet; agree to look for him.

atoms.label: Travel to Eagles' Peak
atoms.detail: West of the Tree Gnome Stronghold. Fairy ring code AKQ is fastest, or a necklace of passage to the Outpost then north-west.

atoms.label: Read the bird book for the metal feather
atoms.detail: At the base tent, Nickolaus is missing. Inspect the books on the ground for a bird book; reading it shakes loose a metal feather.

atoms.label: Open the peak door and shout to Nickolaus
atoms.detail: Climb to the peak (south-side path, or the Agility shortcut beside the tent) to a feather-inscribed rock door; use the metal feather to open it. Inside, take the southern tunnel past the giant feathers to a ledge and shout to Nickolaus, trapped across a chasm by a giant eagle.

atoms.label: Gather 10 eagle feathers
atoms.detail: Piles of giant feathers spawn near where Nickolaus is trapped and throughout the cave.

atoms.label: Have Asyff sew two eagle disguises
atoms.detail: Fancy Dress Shop, south-east Varrock (gated area). Ask Asyff about bird costumes; she makes two eagle capes+beaks from the 10 giant feathers, swamp tar, yellow dye, and 50 coins.

atoms.label: Bring the disguises back to Nickolaus
atoms.detail: Return to the dungeon and shout to him again -- he asks for the disguises to be brought to the eagle door.

atoms.label: Free the bronze feather from its net trap
atoms.detail: West tunnel from the eagle door. Attempting to take the pedestal feather triggers a net trap; operate all four corner winches to break it, then take the feather.

atoms.label: Follow the trail and fight for the silver feather
atoms.detail: Second tunnel. Inspecting the empty pedestal (Hunter 27) reveals a partial trail; follow successive rock-heap inspections until a hidden tunnel is found. A level-13 kebbit attacks -- kill or threaten it for the silver feather (re-searchable from the tunnel if lost).

atoms.label: Solve the bird-seed lever puzzle for the gold feather
atoms.detail: North-east tunnel. Take bird seed from the holder near the entrance (need six placements total). Four levers move five metal eagles blocking the corridor west to the pedestal: centre lever moves east/north-west eagles, east lever moves the north-west eagle, south-west lever moves north-west/south eagles, north-west lever clears the corridor to the feather room. Fill feeders and pull levers in sequence to clear the path, then take the gold feather. A reset lever sits outside the chamber if the puzzle gets stuck.

atoms.label: Use all three feathers and disguise past the guard eagle
atoms.detail: Use the bronze, silver, and gold feathers on the eagle door to open it, then wear the full eagle disguise to walk past the guarding eagle to Nickolaus (skipping the disguise costs minor damage).

atoms.label: Talk to Nickolaus and agree to meet at camp
Learn to catch a ferret from Nickolaus
atoms.detail: Exit the dungeon and find Nickolaus back at the tent; he teaches the capture method (no box trap needed for this scripted catch -- if the ferret is released afterward, a real box trap he provides can recatch it).

atoms.label: Deliver the ferret to Charlie
atoms.detail: Ardougne Zoo. Quest complete.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Swamp Lizards or Barb-tailed Kebbits (box traps) / Swamp lizards / Sapphire glacialis (butterfly net) / Embertailed jerboas.

methods.method: Swamp Lizards or Barb-tailed Kebbits (box traps)
methods.location: Lumbridge Swamp
methods.notes: Row's continuity method; page's own Swamp lizards entry is located north-west of Slepe (not Lumbridge Swamp) and uses rope+small fishing net traps, not box traps; Barb-tailed Kebbits is not named anywhere on the current page.

methods.location: north-west of Slepe
methods.notes: Page states ~20,000-28,000 xp/hr at 29-37 with two traps, rising to ~40,000-45,000 xp/hr with three traps from level 40; ranges left as ?? per single-figure policy.

methods.location: Rellekka hunter area / Farming Guild / Mons Gratia
methods.notes: Page states 28,500 xp/hr at level 25; can be combined with cerulean twitch bird trapping for a slightly higher rate.

methods.location: Locus Oasis
methods.notes: Fairy ring code AJP is adjacent to the hunter area; page states 50,000 xp/hr with three traps.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw box traps
atoms.detail: Withdraw box traps (swamp lizard / kebbit method).

atoms.label: Go to the Lumbridge Swamp hunting ground
atoms.detail: Tile (3209,3165) per the route's own map pin.

atoms.label: Box-trap Swamp Lizards / Barb-tailed Kebbits until Hunter 43
atoms.detail: Lay traps, check them, reset -- loop stays inside this one atom per U4/U7.

atoms.label: Alt: box-trap embertailed jerboas (Locus Oasis)
atoms.detail: Higher xp/hr (50,000, per the row's own method) but requires Eagles' Peak partially completed -- already in this chain's atomized quest set (oppgran:atoms:quest-eagles-peak).

steps.detail: Pick a method: Willow trees / Teak trees.

methods.location: Woodcutting Guild pond, Draynor Village bank, Seers' Village, or Barbarian Outpost
methods.notes: The page's own fastest-xp path actually keeps players on oak until 35 rather than willow, but willow is the well-known casual pick used here for route continuity.

methods.location: Teak-tree areas (e.g. Fossil Island patches)
methods.notes: 34,000 xp/hr is the page's plain no-tick-manipulation rate at level 35; the page calls teak the fastest xp in the game once tick manipulation is added.

atoms.label: Go to the Draynor Village willow trees
atoms.detail: Same bank-adjacent spot as train-woodcutting-30, tile (3088,3255).

atoms.label: Chop willow logs until Woodcutting 45
atoms.detail: Bank or drop logs; this band's stock is a candidate non-GE source for train-firemaking-50's willow-log method noted in that row's own methods[].

steps.detail: Pick a method: Maple logs / Willow logs / Teak logs / Arctic pine logs.

methods.location: Varrock west bank (chop your own maple logs -- never GE)
methods.notes: Matches the row's own label; covers only the final stretch of this 30-50 band per the page's own bracket table. Also the log type required for Desert Treasure per the row's existing detail (that quest req is unverified here, out of scope for this method mining).

methods.location: Varrock west bank (chop your own willow logs -- never GE)
methods.notes: Page's own bracket table entry for the start of this band.

methods.location: Varrock west bank (chop your own teak logs -- never GE)
methods.notes: Page's own bracket table entry for the middle of this band.

methods.location: Varrock west bank (chop your own arctic pine logs -- never GE)
methods.notes: Page's own bracket table entry just before maple logs take over.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw tinderbox + banked maple logs
atoms.detail: Withdraw a tinderbox and maple logs sourced by your own Woodcutting (non-GE) -- the row's own methods[] location field lists 'Grand Exchange' as a burn-spot, not a log source; modelled gather-own regardless per the unconditional hard rule.

atoms.label: Burn maple logs until Firemaking 50
atoms.detail: Required for Desert Treasure per the route's own detail. Any open ground works.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Anvil smithing iron/steel items (iron platebody, then steel warhammer) / Giants' Foundry — forge Iron/Steel alloy swords for Kovac / Blast Furnace — smelt non-gold bars for profit.

hints.note: Superheat Item (Magic 43+) gives passive Smithing xp on the same bars — XREF: training:train-magic-43.

methods.method: Anvil smithing iron/steel items (iron platebody, then steel warhammer)
methods.location: West Varrock anvil
methods.notes: Band spans iron platebody (33-39, 104,200 xp/h) and steel warhammer (39-40, 108,500 xp/h) sub-brackets, no single 30-40 figure, so ??. Row's own Superheat/Magic hint is a separate cross-referenced training row, not modeled as its own method here.

methods.method: Giants' Foundry — forge Iron/Steel alloy swords for Kovac
methods.location: Giants' Foundry minigame
methods.notes: 133,920 xp/h is the guide's stated rate for the iron/steel alloy tier, which unlocks at Smithing 30.

methods.method: Blast Furnace — smelt non-gold bars for profit
methods.location: Blast Furnace, Keldagrim
methods.notes: Same as lower bands: slower but usually profitable; the rate table did not expand to literal numbers in the fetched wikitext.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw hammer + banked iron ore/coal
atoms.detail: Withdraw a hammer and banked iron ore + coal (own-mined, non-GE).

atoms.label: Mine iron ore and coal (non-GE)
atoms.detail: 1 iron ore + 2 coal per steel bar (Steel bar's own Recipe, standard furnace). Site not pinned by the route fixture (??); shares the same ore/coal demand as train-magic-43's Superheat loop earlier in this segment.

atoms.label: Smith steel items at an anvil until Smithing 40
atoms.detail: West Varrock anvil, per the route's own method. Smelt bars into an item (e.g. platebody, then warhammer) at the anvil in one bank cycle.

hints.note: Superheat Item (Magic 43+) gives passive Smithing xp on the same bars -- xref: training:train-magic-43 (same recipe, already in this segment).

steps.detail: Pick a method: Iron ore drop-mining (triangle spots) / Granite 3-tick mining / Motherlode Mine.

methods.location: Any of the 16 three-rock iron triangle sites
methods.notes: Continues the level 15-70 iron route; page's ~45,000-55,000 xp/hr figure is for the whole sub-60 range, not this band specifically.

methods.location: Bandit Camp Quarry or Cape Conch mine
methods.notes: Only unlocks at the very end of this band (level 45); page's own xp table starts at level 45 (87,000 xp/hr with full setup).

methods.location: Motherlode Mine, near the Mining Guild in Falador
methods.notes: Low-effort profitable alternative available from level 30; page only gives an xp/hr figure at level 90, so none is cited for this band.

atoms.label: Equip best available pickaxe
atoms.detail: Equip (or withdraw+equip) the best pickaxe your Mining/Attack level allows.

atoms.label: Drop-mine iron ore until Mining 45
atoms.detail: Any of the 16 three-rock iron triangle sites named by the row's own method; exact site not pinned by the route fixture (??). Banked iron ore here is the same non-GE stock train-smithing-40/train-magic-43 in this segment consume.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Green dragonhide body / Diamond ring / gem cutting continuation / Water battlestaff.

methods.location: Craft tanned green dragon leather into bodies with a needle and thread (or costume needle) at a bank.
methods.notes: MISMATCH: the row's continuity method names green dragonhide bodies for this 40-54 band, but the page states green d'hide bodies actually require Crafting 63, well above this band's ceiling -- flagged rather than silently re-banded.

methods.location: Continue cutting uncut gems (ruby, then diamond) to sell or use in jewellery.
methods.notes: Ruby unlocks at 34, diamond at 43, both inside this band; page's own text recommends continuing gem cutting up to level 62 before switching methods.

methods.location: Craft water battlestaves per the page's battlestaff table.
methods.notes: Water battlestaff opens at exactly level 54, the band's tail end; the fetched section doesn't name the crafting components so reqs.items is left empty rather than guessed. Page's per-hour figures use a templated crafted-per-hour assumption, not one stated number.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw needle + thread
atoms.detail: Withdraw a needle and thread (or costume needle).

atoms.label: Kill green dragons for dragonhide (non-GE)
atoms.detail: Green dragons (combat level 79, Wilderness) drop Green dragonhide 100% of the time, per the monster's own drop table. Sites named by the wiki: east of the Boneyard Hunter area (3331,3672), west of Dark Warriors' Fortress (2973,3620), north of the Graveyard of Shadows (3137,3707), south of the Lava Maze (3078,3810) -- all deep Wilderness, PK risk.

hints.note: Wilderness PK risk -- hop if contested or under threat.
Same kill also drops Dragon bones (100%) -- xref: training:train-prayer-52 later in this segment can share this exact activity.

atoms.label: Tan green dragonhide into green dragon leather (named vendor, non-GE)
atoms.detail: Ellis, the Al Kharid tanner (tile 3274,3192), tans green dragonhide at the standard 20gp/hide price -- a named NPC service, not the Grand Exchange.

atoms.label: Craft green dragonhide bodies until Crafting 54
atoms.detail: Sew tanned green dragon leather into bodies with a needle and thread at a bank.

steps.detail: Grants access to Ape Atoll and Maniacal Monkey tunnels.

hints.note: start: ask about the missing 10th squad
Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Accept King Narnode's request
atoms.detail: Speak to King Narnode Shareen at the Grand Tree and agree to investigate the missing 10th squad. Keep an inventory slot free or the gnome royal seal drops on the ground.

hints.note: start: ask about the missing 10th squad

atoms.label: Glide to Gandius
atoms.detail: Climb up the tree at the Grand Tree and take the gnome glider to Gandius.

atoms.label: Get monkey bones if needed
atoms.detail: If you don't already have monkey bones, kill a monkey nearby for some (if Tai Bwo Wannai Trio is already started, only a monkey corpse drops, which also works).

atoms.label: Enter the Shipyard gate
atoms.detail: Enter the Shipyard gate to the north, carrying the Royal Seal.

atoms.label: Speak with G.L.O. Caranock
atoms.detail: Speak with G.L.O. Caranock in the building south of the Shipyard gate.

atoms.label: Report back to King Narnode
atoms.detail: Return to King Narnode and speak to him. Keep 1 free inventory slot, or Narnode's orders drop to the floor.

atoms.label: Talk through every option with Daero
atoms.detail: Talk to Daero in the bar on the first floor (east side) of the Grand Tree, working through every dialogue option in turn, to be teleported to a new location.

atoms.label: Talk to Daero inside the hangar
atoms.detail: Talk (not "travel") to Daero again, now inside the hangar.

atoms.label: Solve the hangar sliding puzzle
atoms.detail: Click the panel in the south-eastern hangar and solve the sliding puzzle, or alternatively pay Glough back at the Stronghold to solve it for you.

atoms.label: Fly to Crash Island
atoms.detail: Watch the cutscene, then talk to Daero, then to Waydar, to fly to Crash Island.

hints.note: Yes

atoms.label: Speak to Lumdo
atoms.detail: Talk to Lumdo on Crash Island.

atoms.label: Have Waydar convince Lumdo
atoms.detail: Talk (not "travel") to Waydar twice so he convinces Lumdo to follow orders.

hints.note: I cannot convince Lumdo to take us to the island.

atoms.label: Sail to Ape Atoll
atoms.detail: Talk to Lumdo again to travel west to Ape Atoll.

atoms.label: Enter the valley and get arrested
atoms.detail: Follow the red path into the valley on Ape Atoll. You'll be shot and arrested by the guards.

atoms.label: Pick the cell lock and escape
atoms.detail: Watch the guards' patrol pattern, pick the lock on your cell door with a lockpick, and escape. Stay at least 2 squares away from the guards, even while inside the cell.

atoms.label: Talk to Garkor
atoms.detail: Follow the green path and talk to Garkor, completing the full dialogue (skipping it means Zooknock won't give the m'speak amulet later).

atoms.label: Find the monkey dentures
atoms.detail: Follow the blue path to the U-shaped building and enter through the southern door, staying on the dark-brown floor to avoid alerting guards. Search the stacked crates on the light-brown floor for Monkey dentures.

atoms.label: Fall into the cave below
atoms.detail: Heal up first (fall damage applies), then search the south-eastern crate to fall into a cave below.

atoms.label: Find the M'amulet mould
atoms.detail: Search the north-western crates in the cave for a M'amulet mould, then you may teleport away safely.

atoms.label: Mine a gold ore
atoms.detail: Mine a gold ore (Mining 40, pickaxe) at a gold rock. Exact nearest rock to the quest's travel path is unconfirmed this pass ("??") — any gold rock works, this is a generic material, not a quest-location-specific one.

atoms.label: Smelt the gold bar
atoms.detail: Smelt the gold ore into a gold bar at any furnace (Smithing 40, boostable), or cast Superheat Item (Magic 43) to skip the furnace trip.

atoms.label: Shear a sheep for wool
atoms.detail: Shear a sheep with a pair of shears to collect wool — any sheep pen near your bank works (e.g. Lumbridge or Falador).

atoms.label: Spin the wool into a ball
atoms.detail: Spin the wool into a ball of wool on a spinning wheel (Crafting 1).

atoms.label: Descend into the Ape Atoll Dungeon
atoms.detail: Resupply at a bank (good food, prayer/antipoison/stamina potions), return to Ape Atoll via Daero, Waydar, and Lumdo, follow the yellow path west, and climb down the ladder into the Ape Atoll Dungeon.

atoms.label: Fight through to Zooknock
atoms.detail: Run through the dungeon to Zooknock, keeping Protect from Melee active against the level-98 Monkey Zombies and level-142 Skeletons, eating and drinking potions as needed.

atoms.label: Speak to Zooknock
atoms.detail: Talk to Zooknock, the exiled monkey blacksmith.

atoms.label: Have Zooknock forge the enchanted bar
atoms.detail: Use your Monkey dentures, M'amulet mould, and gold bar on Zooknock, then talk to him to receive the Enchanted bar and get your mould back. Do not give him the mould a second time once you already have the bar, or he'll keep it.

atoms.label: Reach the temple with the wall of flames
atoms.detail: Teleport away then return (Daero, Waydar, Lumdo). Follow the red path, get arrested and escape again, then follow the pink path to the temple. Bearded guards can't be walked through — enter the trapdoor, then navigate via the ladder to avoid the gorillas.

atoms.label: Forge the M'speak amulet
atoms.detail: With the M'amulet mould in your inventory, use the Enchanted bar with the wall of flames, then string the result with your ball of wool to create the M'speak amulet.

atoms.label: Head to the Monkey child's house
atoms.detail: Follow the pink path back to the jail, then the cyan path to the Monkey child's house. Watch for the Monkey's Aunt — it's only safe in the garden while she's away.

atoms.label: Convince the monkey child
atoms.detail: Equip the M'speak amulet and talk to the monkey child, then talk to him twice more to trigger "Well I'll be a monkey's uncle!"

hints.note: Well I'll be a monkey's uncle! -> How many bananas did Aunty want?

atoms.label: Pick 5 bananas
atoms.detail: Pick 5 bananas from the trees in the garden.

atoms.label: Deliver the bananas
atoms.detail: Give the bananas to the monkey child, leave the garden, wait for the aunt to pass through, then talk to him again to receive a Monkey talisman.

atoms.label: Return to Zooknock for the greegree
atoms.detail: Teleport away then return (Daero, Waydar, Lumdo), resupply at a bank, and follow the yellow path down into the Ape Atoll Dungeon again.

atoms.label: Trade for a greegree
atoms.detail: Give Zooknock a Monkey talisman and Monkey bones from a regular monkey (not another type) to receive a Greegree. Be careful not to accidentally bury the bones, and don't leave the Zooknock dialogue early or you'll have to restart the cutscene.

atoms.label: Prepare at the Ardougne Zoo
atoms.detail: Head to the Ardougne Zoo, equip the Karamjan monkey greegree, and talk to the Monkey minder (click through any first-time dialogue).

atoms.label: Bag a monkey
atoms.detail: Equip the M'speak amulet and talk to a nearby monkey so it jumps into your bag. Remove the greegree and speak to the minder again.

atoms.label: Walk back to Ape Atoll on foot
atoms.detail: Travel by foot (walk or run) back to the Grand Tree — do not use any teleportation method — then return to Ape Atoll by talking to Daero, Waydar, and Lumdo.

atoms.label: Reach King Awowogei
atoms.detail: Equip the M'speak amulet and Karamjan monkey greegree — Ape Atoll is no longer hostile while wearing it. Follow the red path through the gates, talk to Garkor, then the nearby Elder Guard, then head to the west coast, climb the hill, cross the bridge, and talk to Kruk.

atoms.label: Speak with King Awowogei
atoms.detail: Talk to Awowogei twice, then talk to the Elder Guard again to be let out.

atoms.label: Receive the 10th squad sigil
atoms.detail: Talk to Garkor and watch the cutscene, then talk to him again to receive the 10th squad sigil.

atoms.label: Prepare for the Jungle Demon
atoms.detail: Prepare combat equipment for the final fight — good food, prayer potions, and high-healing food are strongly recommended.

atoms.label: Defeat the Jungle Demon
atoms.detail: Equip the 10th squad sigil, then fight the Jungle Demon. Use Protect from Magic and stay out of melee range to minimise damage.

atoms.label: Report victory to King Narnode
atoms.detail: Return to the Grand Tree and talk to King Narnode Shareen to complete the quest.

checkpoints.label: Well I'll be a monkey's uncle

steps.detail: Pick a method: Offer dragon bones at a gilded altar / Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar / Reanimate ensouled heads near the Dark Altar.

methods.method: Offer dragon bones at a gilded altar
methods.location: player-owned house gilded altar (or a hosted altar, e.g. worlds 330/465/512 at Rimmington)
methods.notes: Page states 643,000 xp/hr for dragon bones with both incense burners lit; a hosted altar removes the Construction 75 requirement.

methods.method: Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar
methods.location: Chaos Temple, level 38 Wilderness (multi-combat, player-killer risk)
methods.notes: Page states up to 860,000 xp/hr bringing noted dragon bones (500,000 xp/hr if suiciding each trip instead); cheaper than the gilded altar but carries PK risk.

methods.method: Reanimate ensouled heads near the Dark Altar
methods.location: Arceuus, Dark Altar (fairy ring code CIS)
methods.notes: Slower, safer alternative fed by ensouled heads gathered as monster drops rather than bought; page only states a concrete hourly rate for the high-Magic ensouled dragon head option.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw dragon bones
atoms.detail: Withdraw banked dragon bones (gathered non-GE, see next atom).

atoms.label: Kill green dragons for dragon bones (non-GE)
atoms.detail: Green dragons (Wilderness, combat 79) drop Dragon bones 100% of the time, per the monster's own drop table -- shares the exact same kill activity as train-crafting-54's dragonhide gather earlier in this segment (both drops fire from one kill).

hints.note: Wilderness PK risk -- hop if contested or under threat.

atoms.label: Offer dragon bones at a player-owned house gilded altar
atoms.detail: Best xp/hr (643,000) of the row's own 3 methods, but gated behind Construction 75 for the altar itself.

atoms.label: Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar (Wilderness)
atoms.detail: Level 38 Wilderness, multi-combat, PK risk -- no Construction requirement. Highest raw xp/hr (860,000) of the 3 methods.

hints.note: Wilderness multi-combat PK risk -- hop if contested.

atoms.label: Reanimate ensouled heads near the Dark Altar (Arceuus)
atoms.detail: No Construction requirement -- the safe fallback for an account without a gilded altar yet. Fairy ring code CIS per the row's own method.

checkpoints.label: Dragon-bone gather + altar offering, routing branch (to 52)

steps.detail: Pick a method: High Level Alchemy / Superheat Item (smelt ores into bars) / Arceuus Library book-delivery minigame / Enchant Crossbow Bolt (highest affordable gem bolts, e.g. Ruby).

hints.note: 65xp/cast, up to 78,000xp/h stationary [ref: Pay-to-play Magic training s14].

methods.location: Anywhere, on noted items
methods.notes: Core money-maker from level 55; up to ~78,000 xp/h stationary per the page, matching this row's own hint. Per project convention, source items to alch via drops/production, not Grand Exchange purchase.

methods.location: Anywhere with ore stock (bank noted ore)
methods.notes: Viable option for the pre-55 portion of this band; also grants passive Smithing xp.

methods.location: Arceuus Library, Great Kourend
methods.notes: No hard minimum level per the page; rune-free xp via delivering requested books, best done with a book-location RuneLite plugin. Page's ~200,000 xp/h figure is quoted at level 75, not this band, so left unsourced here.

methods.method: Enchant Crossbow Bolt (highest affordable gem bolts, e.g. Ruby)
methods.location: Anywhere, on owned bolts
methods.notes: Highest-speed option for players prioritising xp/h over cost.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw High Level Alchemy loadout
atoms.detail: Withdraw nature runes (1/cast) and fire runes (5/cast) per High Level Alchemy's own cost table, plus own-produced smithed items as alch fodder (steel items from train-smithing-40 / bars from train-magic-43, both earlier in this segment) -- never GE-bought fodder.

atoms.label: Cast High Level Alchemy on own-produced items until Magic 55
atoms.detail: 1 nature rune + 5 fire runes per cast, per High Level Alchemy's own cost table. Fodder = the chain's own smithed/superheated output rather than a bought batch -- gold value of what's alched stays ?? (not fabricated).

hints.note: 65xp/cast, up to 78,000xp/h stationary, per the route's own hint.
Alch the chain's own steel items (train-smithing-40) or superheated bars (train-magic-43) rather than buying fodder -- non-GE loop per the wave3:consumable-loops queue note.

atoms.label: Alt: Enchant Crossbow Bolt (ruby bolts)
atoms.detail: 324,500 xp/hr per the row's own method -- highest of the 4 cited alternates, gated at Magic 49.

checkpoints.label: High Level Alchemy on own-produced fodder (to 55)

steps.detail: Pick a method: Ancient Magicks bursting (Smoke Burst, then Ice Burst) / High Level Alchemy / Exploiting elemental weakness on crabs (Frost Crabs, 100% Fire weakness) / Tele-alching (High Level Alchemy chained with a teleport).

hints.note: Smoke Burst usable from 62; Ice Burst better from 70 — exact xp/h not sourced.

methods.method: Ancient Magicks bursting (Smoke Burst, then Ice Burst)
methods.location: Multi-combat spawn (per-monster; page does not list a crab-specific burst spot)
methods.notes: Smoke Burst usable from 62, Ice Burst stronger from 70 once Desert Treasure I unlocks Ancient Magicks; the page's own burst-location list (Slayer/Maniacal monkeys/Zombie pirates/Bandits) does not include crabs despite this row's 'at Crabs' label.

methods.location: Anywhere, on noted items
methods.notes: The 'Alching' half of this row's label; up to ~78,000 xp/h stationary. Per project convention, source items to alch via drops/production, not Grand Exchange purchase.

methods.method: Exploiting elemental weakness on crabs (Frost Crabs, 100% Fire weakness)
methods.location: Ruins of Tapoyauik, Varlamore
methods.notes: Closest documented actual crab-killing option matching this row's 'at Crabs' phrasing; Fire spells hit roughly double damage on the 100% weakness.

methods.method: Tele-alching (High Level Alchemy chained with a teleport)
methods.location: Camelot or Ardougne teleport spot
methods.notes: Cast High Alchemy then a teleport within 3 ticks so both grant xp; Camelot is the cheapest routing at ~144,600 xp/h, Ardougne is faster but pricier per the page.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw High Level Alchemy loadout
atoms.detail: Same loadout as train-magic-55's tmg55-01 (nature rune 1/cast + fire rune 5/cast) plus own-produced fodder from earlier smithing/farming lanes -- continuing the exact non-GE alch loop past its 55 threshold rather than minting a second one (U8).

atoms.label: Cast High Level Alchemy on own-produced items until Magic 70
atoms.detail: Continuation of tmg55-02's loop (own-produced fodder, never GE-bought) with the until-threshold raised to 70. 65xp/cast, up to 78,000xp/h stationary per Pay-to-play_Magic_training.s14.

hints.note: Same non-GE fodder loop as tmg55-02 -- alch the chain's own smithed/farmed output, never a bought batch.

atoms.label: Alt: Ancient Magicks bursting (dust devils, Catacombs of Kourend)
atoms.detail: Smoke Burst usable from Magic 62 (Ancient Magicks, requires Desert Treasure I); dust devils in the Catacombs of Kourend named as a documented cheap alternative to maniacal monkeys for players without Monkey Madness II, needing 65 Slayer. Corrects the coarse row's own atom.target='crab' -- 'crab' is not documented anywhere in Pay-to-play_Magic_training.s17's own bursting-spot list (maniacal monkeys, zombie pirates, bandits, dust devils, Temple Spiders, TzHaar-Ket) nor is the page's only crab-adjacent method (Frost Crabs, s8) valid for this band: s8's own section header states 'Levels 1-55', not 55-70 as the coarse row's methods[] mislabels it.

hints.note: No dust-devil-specific xp/hr figure stated on Pay-to-play_Magic_training.s17; left ?? rather than guessed.

checkpoints.label: High Level Alchemy on own-produced fodder (continued to 70), Ancient Magicks bursting optional

steps.detail: Pick a method: Moss giant / Nightmare Zone (Normal Rumble) / Wilderness Slayer / Ogre.

hints.note: No single wiki-cited monster fetched for this exact band; Nightmare Zone (needs 5 quests, requirement list unverified) is the wiki's documented 50-99 AFK/fastest method.

methods.location: Catacombs of Kourend, Varrock Sewers, or west of the Fishing Guild
methods.notes: Fair Hitpoints for its level with a chance at a Mossy key (Bryophyta access); hits hard on low-Defence/low-armour players.

methods.location: North of Yanille, north-west of the bank

quests: Any 5 of the ~30 quests Nightmare Zone accepts (see Nightmare_Zone.s3 for the full list)

methods.notes: Instanced and safe (deaths only drop arena loot); absorption/overload potions from points reduce food/potion costs; entry needs a coin fee.

methods.location: Wilderness
methods.notes: Slayer helm accuracy/damage bonuses make Wilderness Slayer assignments good melee xp while also banking Slayer points quickly; player-vs-player danger applies.

methods.location: East of Castle Wars, west of Yanille
methods.notes: High-Hitpoints, low-Defence target with automatic aggro; a popular cannon spot so can be crowded.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw Moss Giant loadout
atoms.detail: Withdraw food ??x and best-available weapon/armour for the attack style being trained.

atoms.label: Go to Moss giants (west of the Fishing Guild)
atoms.detail: Surface spot, members-only, no key/quest gate (unlike the Catacombs of Kourend or Brimhaven Dungeon alternatives on the same page).

atoms.label: Kill Moss giants until Attack 70
atoms.detail: Always drops big bones (bury for Prayer XP or bank for the prayer-pot-supply chain). Level 42, max hit 6, Crush attack, 50% Fire weakness.

hints.note: Big bones always drop -- bank for train-prayer-74's dragon-bones-adjacent Prayer training or bury on the spot.
Popular F2P/early-P2P training spot; hop worlds if contested.
Trains attack/strength/defence simultaneously depending on combat style used -- the coarse route models these as three sequential bands, but a real player likely trains all three concurrently here.

atoms.label: Bank: deposit Moss Giant loot
atoms.detail: Deposit big bones, coins, and any weapon/armour drops.

atoms.label: Alt: Wilderness Slayer tasks
atoms.detail: Wilderness Slayer trains combat and Slayer simultaneously (see train-slayer-50 later in this same segment) but carries PK risk in the Wilderness.

hints.note: No xp/hr figure stated on Pay-to-play_Melee_training.s14 for this method.

checkpoints.label: Moss giant routing (west of Fishing Guild)

steps.detail: Pick a method: Moss giant / Wilderness Slayer / Nightmare Zone (Normal Rumble).

methods.location: Catacombs of Kourend, Varrock Sewers, or west of the Fishing Guild
methods.notes: Fair Hitpoints for its level with a chance at a Mossy key (Bryophyta access); hits hard on low-Defence/low-armour players.

methods.location: Wilderness
methods.notes: Slayer helm accuracy/damage bonuses make Wilderness Slayer assignments good melee xp while also banking Slayer points quickly; player-vs-player danger applies.

methods.location: North of Yanille, north-west of the bank

quests: Any 5 of the ~30 quests Nightmare Zone accepts (see Nightmare_Zone.s3 for the full list)

methods.notes: Instanced and safe (deaths only drop arena loot); absorption/overload potions from points reduce food/potion costs; entry needs a coin fee.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw Moss Giant loadout
atoms.detail: Withdraw food ??x and best-available weapon/armour for the attack style being trained.

atoms.label: Go to Moss giants (west of the Fishing Guild)
